title: Garlicky Intense Norwegian Lamb Stew
description: A hearty, garlicky lamb stew originating from Norway, perfect for the holiday season. This dish combines tender lamb with a rich, creamy sauce and a hint of capers.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter to the pot and sauté the onion until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, caraway seeds, and thyme to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the flour to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the beef broth to the pot, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Return the lamb to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2-3 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the capers and heavy cream. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh parsley.
Let the stew rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld.
